title: Argentinian Winey Creamy Midnight Craving Casserole
description: A hearty, comforting casserole featuring the flavors of Argentina. Perfect for a late-night craving.
instructions: Grease a 9x13 inch baking dish with olive oil.
In a large skillet, cook the prosciutto over medium heat until crispy, then set aside.
In the same skillet, sauté the minced garlic until fragrant, then add the red wine and cook until the liquid has reduced by half.
In a separate bowl, mix together the grated Gruyère cheese, chopped parsley, and cooked prosciutto.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of salt.
Add the cooked garlic and wine mixture to the egg mixture, then stir in the flour to thicken the sauce.
Arrange the sliced Braeburn apples in the bottom of the prepared baking dish.
Pour the egg and cheese mixture over the apples, then dot the top with additional Gruyère cheese.
Transfer the baking dish to the preheated oven and bake for 35 minutes, or until the casserole is golden brown and set.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
